description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, flat base with a prominent, tilted solar panel array. The main solar panel is mounted at an angle, supported by a robust structural frame, and is accompanied by several smaller geometric components, possibly representing electrical housings or auxiliary equipment. The surface of the base is textured, with grid-like patterns suggesting photovoltaic cells and technical detailing. There are cylindrical and rectangular modules distributed across the base, likely representing inverters, junction boxes, or other electrical components. The overall design is industrial and functional, with a focus on maximizing solar exposure and efficient energy conversion. The structure appears to be intended for ground or flat-roof mounting in an urban environment.
description: A large, angled solar panel array mounted on a textured base with auxiliary electrical components and technical detailing, designed for urban solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent triangular tilt of the main solar panel and the arrangement of auxiliary modules on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep angle of the solar panel and the elongated, rectangular base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backside of the tilted panel, with visible support structures and additional technical modules.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the length of the base and the angular support of the solar panel.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) support frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large panels with auxiliary modules distributed on the base
